title: Evangelion 20 and North America
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.0 and North America             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Mon Nov 29, 2010 8:47 pm [url]http://blog.funimation.com/2010/11/evangelion-2-0-you-can-not-advance-attacks-north-america/[/url] [url]http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/evangelion-theatrical-release-49390[/url] Seems Cinema Asia and Eleven Arts will be playing a substantial role in this, along with familiar face and English-audio provider FUNimation Entertainment. We will have action this-coming January.

[]:             Quote Post by Naminator » Mon Nov 29, 2010 9:42 pm Awwwwww yeaaaahh!

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Mon Nov 29, 2010 11:14 pm Yes! I hope it comes to my city or at the least, to a plausibly close theater near me.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 29, 2010 11:52 pm I don't understand. We already know this. How is this news?

[]:             Quote Post by Zekkykins » Mon Nov 29, 2010 11:58 pm Now we have a date? Did we have a date before?

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Tue Nov 30, 2010 12:05 am if this means its coming to Fresno theatrically, bring it on! :)

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Tue Nov 30, 2010 12:11 am The Eva Monkey wrote: I don't understand. We already know this. How is this news?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Nov 30, 2010 9:46 am Hatsumi92 wrote: I posted this like a week or so ago when it was announced in ANN's Press Release section. I posted it in the DVD/BD release date thread aside from making a thread just for the date. I guess hardly anyone saw it.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Nov 30, 2010 10:32 am The date is news to me. This is friggin' awesome!

[]:             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Tue Nov 30, 2010 12:51 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I don't understand. We already know this. How is this news?

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Tue Nov 30, 2010 12:59 pm I'm psyced that 2.0 will be coming to the states soon! Finally, I'll get to see what every one has been bitching about for the past year!

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Nov 30, 2010 1:03 pm I don't see a date here, I just see a month. That's 31 different possible dates. Did I just glance over an actual theatrical release date (i.w. January 7) or is it still the vague month?

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Tue Nov 30, 2010 2:55 pm I already tweeted about this. Also, those blogs got that info directly from ANN. http://twitter.com/evageeks/status/6961534383165440

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Nov 30, 2010 9:37 pm I hope to see the listing of theaters this is being shown in very soon Can't wait!

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 30, 2010 10:26 pm One silly question that I'd like to ask: is it shown in the theatres dubbed or subbed? I never watched an anime in your country and I never heard about this detail.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Tue Nov 30, 2010 10:29 pm I think whether a foreign film is showed subtitled or dubbed really depends on the theater and the distributor more than anything else; there's not really one standard for it that everyone abides by. I'd assume that if Funimation has their dub ready to go in time, though, they'd want to use it for the showings.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Nov 30, 2010 10:50 pm I am not sure the dub is supposed to be a part of 2.22 on March 29, 2011, so the chances are 50/50 as to dub or sub for theaters I say 50/50 because theaters showing foreign films usually go with the sub of a film unless it is a film from a major studio, i.e. Enter the Dragon, Crouching Tiger, all Ghibli films, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Nov 30, 2010 10:57 pm Thank you for the explanation; here it's not shown in the theatres at all, it's only projected for some events (which are done subbed when the dubs aren't available and dubbed when they're ready). I was curious to know how things work out on the other side of the Atlantic.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Nov 30, 2010 11:03 pm The dubbed/subbed foreign films in the theatre, at least in my experience, seem to depend quite a bit on how it's being marketed and who it's being marketed to.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Tue Nov 30, 2010 11:54 pm Because of how Funimation run, it'll be dubbed. They said something about "if the screening is by Funimation, it'll be dubbed. But at film festivals in the future it'll be subbed" but that was about Summer Wars. No doubt that goes for Evangelion too.
summary: []: Evangelion 2.0 and North America             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Mon Nov 29, 2010 8:47 pm [url]http://blog.funimation.com/2010/11/evangelion-2-0-you-can-not-advance-attacks-north-america/[/url] [url]http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/evangelion-theatrical-release-49390[/url] Seems Cinema Asia and Eleven Arts will be playing a substantial role in this, along with familiar face and English-audio provider FUNimation Entertainment
